expectations.text: Recognizes audit_event as a transactional / continuously-growing table
expectations.evidence: 'This is a transactional table... append-only history that grows without bound'

expectations.text: Recommends an indexed date filter / bounded time window for queries on it
expectations.evidence: Articulates smell #4: 'a query on this table without a time window will read history that only grows; the time filter keeps work proportional'; pattern (b) keeps created_at bound on indexed col

expectations.text: Proposes composite indexes for the stated access patterns and addresses column order (e.g. (user_id, created_at), (type, created_at))
expectations.evidence: (user_id, created_at DESC) and (type, created_at DESC) with explicit equality-leads-range column-order reasoning

expectations.text: Mentions the write/storage cost of indexes or warns against over-indexing (a tradeoff)
expectations.evidence: 'Index restraint... every index taxes the write path, and this is a write-heavy table'
